methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $16M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Curio Solutions is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Ed McGinnis and Pete Zweifel, having raised $16M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 peers across nuclear energy—3 public (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x multiplier to the $16M raised proxy, a standard step-up multiple for Series A to next-stage advancement, yielding $32M. Peer-median triangulation was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comps in the narrow nuclear energy cohort; while 8 private peers exist in the sector, fewer than three clustered at comparable stages or valuation tiers, making a peer-based cross-check unreliable and potentially misleading.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $27M (low) to $37M (high), with a median point of $32M. This range reflects conservative (−15%) and upside (+15%) scenarios around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the thinness of same-stage comparable data in nuclear energy, the heterogeneity of business models and technology maturity across the peer set, and absence of near-term revenue or profitability data to anchor relative valuation. A Series B fundraise with demonstrated technical or commercial progress would enable more robust peer-based triangulation.
